skill.name: background-removal
skill.description: Remove image backgrounds with AI via verging.ai. Supports local files and remote URLs. Returns transparent PNG.
skill.summary: AI-powered background removal skill that calls verging.ai API. Removes backgrounds from JPG, PNG, and WebP images up to 10MB, returning transparent PNG results.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: This skill consists of markdown documentation (README.md and SKILL.md) describing how to use the verging.ai API for AI-powered background removal. The static analyzer flagged 60 patterns including backtick execution, hardcoded URLs, API key references, weak cryptography, and dangerous combinations — all of which are false positives when evaluated in context. The backtick matches are bash code blocks in markdown documentation showing curl commands, not executable Ruby or shell code. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation links to verging.ai. API key references document the correct pattern of using the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable. No cryptographic code exists. The combination heuristics triggered because curl (network) plus env var (credentials) plus temp directory (filesystem) appear together, but this is standard practice for any API-based image processing skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation-only skill with no executable code
low_findings.description: The skill contains only markdown documentation (README.md and SKILL.md) with curl command examples. While static analysis flagged 60 patterns, all are false positives when semantic context is considered. The skill documents API usage patterns but does not ship executable code that would run automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both files are pure markdown with no executable code. All security patterns are documentation examples, not actual code execution paths.

low_findings.title: Network calls to external API (verging.ai)
low_findings.description: The skill documents curl commands that send requests to verging.ai API endpoints. This is expected behavior for an API wrapper skill, but users should be aware that images are uploaded to a third-party service for processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence in the documentation: curl commands to verging.ai/api/v1/ endpoints. This is intentional API integration, not data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: API key handling via environment variable
low_findings.description: The skill requires VERGING_API_KEY environment variable for authentication. This is the correct security practice — keys are never hardcoded and are passed via Authorization header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill correctly uses environment variables for API key storage. Documentation explicitly warns users to never expose API keys in public repos.

low_findings.title: Temp directory usage for image downloads
low_findings.description: The skill documents using /tmp/verging-bg-removal/ for temporary image storage during processing. This is a dedicated subdirectory and standard practice for image processing workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Temp directory usage is documented and uses a dedicated subdirectory. Documentation states temp files are cleaned up after use.

content.user_title: Remove image backgrounds instantly with AI
content.value_statement: Removing image backgrounds manually is time-consuming and requires expensive software. This skill automates the process using the verging.ai API, delivering transparent PNG results from any JPG, PNG, or WebP file.
actual_capabilities: Removes backgrounds from JPG, PNG, and WebP images up to 10MB
Returns transparent PNG output suitable for compositing
Processes both local files and remote image URLs
Polls job status automatically until processing completes
Validates file format and size before upload
limitations: Requires a verging.ai API key and account credits
Maximum file size is 10MB per image
Only supports JPG, PNG, and WebP input formats
Processing time depends on verging.ai server load and job queue

use_cases.title: E-commerce product photography
use_cases.description: Remove backgrounds from product photos to create clean white-background listings or transparent overlays for marketing materials.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce store owners and product photographers

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: Quickly extract subjects from photos for memes, profile pictures, or social media graphics without opening Photoshop.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Batch image processing automation
use_cases.description: Integrate background removal into automated workflows via Claude Code to process multiple images programmatically.

prompt_templates.title: Remove background from a local image
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from /path/to/photo.jpg and save the result as /path/to/photo-transparent.png
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic usage with a local file

prompt_templates.title: Process a remote image URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from https://example.com/product-photo.jpg and download the transparent result

prompt_templates.title: Batch process multiple images
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove backgrounds from all JPG files in /path/to/photos/ and save results with -transparent suffix in the same directory
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple images in one request

prompt_templates.title: Check credits before processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my verging.ai credit balance and then remove the background from /path/to/portrait.png

output_examples.input: Remove background from https://example.com/headshot.png and download the result

best_practices: Store your API key in the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable rather than hardcoding it in scripts
Validate image format and size before submitting to avoid wasted API calls
Use the dedicated /tmp/verging-bg-removal/ directory for temporary files to keep your workspace organized
anti_patterns: Never commit your VERGING_API_KEY to version control or share it publicly
Do not attempt to upload images larger than 10MB — the API will reject them and waste credits
Avoid using Bearer token format when ApiKey format is available — prefer the canonical authentication method

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JPG, JPEG, PNG, and WebP formats. Maximum file size is 10MB per image.

faq.question: How much does each background removal cost?
faq.answer: Each image processing costs 1 credit from your verging.ai account. Pricing is fixed regardless of image size or complexity.

faq.question: Do I need to upload the image separately before creating a job?
faq.answer: No. The create-job endpoint accepts multipart file upload directly via the image parameter. The server handles storage internally.

faq.question: How long does processing take?
faq.answer: Processing time varies based on server load, but the skill polls the job status endpoint every 5 seconds and returns the result as soon as it completes.

faq.question: Can I process images from remote URLs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill downloads remote images to a temporary directory before processing. Use the --download flag to auto-download the result.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit verging.ai, log in, click your avatar in the top right, and navigate to API Keys. Create a new key and set it as the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable.
